Title: User Registration and Login
Test Data:
- Username: testuser
- Password: testpassword
- Email: test@example.com
- Admin: false
- Footprint_totale: "0 kg CO2"
- New password: newpassword
- New email: newemail@example.com
- New admin status: true
- New footprint: "10 kg CO2"
- User is not registered in the database
Test Steps:
1. User enters username, password, email, and sets admin status to false.
2. User submits the form to register.
3. Application sends a request to the database to verify registration.
4. Database responds with "not registered".
5. Application sends the homepage to the user.
6. User logs out.
7. User attempts to log in with the same credentials.
8. Application sends a request to the database to verify registration.
9. Database responds with "registered".
10. Application sends the homepage to the user.
Expected Result:
- User is successfully registered and logged in.
- User can change their details and log out.

Title: User Login with Invalid Credentials
Test Steps:
1. User enters an invalid username and password.
2. User submits the form to log in.
3. Application sends a request to the database to verify registration.
4. Database responds with "not registered".
Expected Result: - User is not logged in and sees an error message.

Title: Production Data Modification
Preconditions: User is logged in and has access to production data.
Test Data:
- ID: 1
- Utente: testuser
- Modello: model1
- Footprint produzione: "5 kg CO2"
- Footprint totale: "10 kg CO2"
- New footprint produzione: "7 kg CO2"
- New footprint totale: "12 kg CO2"
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the production data modification page.
2. User selects the production entry with ID 1.
3. User updates the footprint produzione to "7 kg CO2".
4. User updates the footprint totale to "12 kg CO2".
5. User saves the changes.
6. Application sends a request to the database to update the data.
7. Database confirms the update.
8. Application sends a confirmation message to the user.
Expected Result:
- Production data is updated successfully.
- User receives a confirmation message.

Title: Attempt to Modify Non-Existent Production Data
Preconditions: User is logged in and has access to production data.
Test Data:
- ID: 999
- Utente: testuser
- Modello: nonexistentmodel
- Footprint produzione: "5 kg CO2"
- Footprint totale: "10 kg CO2"
- New footprint produzione: "7 kg CO2"
- New footprint totale: "12 kg CO2"
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the production data modification page.
2. User selects the production entry with ID 999.
3. User tries to update the footprint produzione to "7 kg CO2".
4. User tries to update the footprint totale to "12 kg CO2".
5. User saves the changes.
6. Application sends a request to the database to update the data.
7. Database responds with an error indicating the data does not exist.
Expected Result:
- User is informed that the production data does not exist.
- No changes are made to the database.

Title: Device Scan with Functional Components
Preconditions: User is logged in and has access to device scanning.
Test Data:
- Component name: ComponentA
- Impact: Low
- Diagnostics: Healthy
Test Steps:
1. User initiates the device scan.
2. Application performs the hardware scan.
3. Database returns the component name, impact, and diagnostics.
4. Application displays the results to the user.
Expected Result:
- User receives a report with the component name, impact, and diagnostics.
- The component is marked as functional.

Title: Device Scan with Defective Components
Preconditions: User is logged in and has access to device scanning.
Test Data:
- Component name: ComponentB
- Impact: High
- Diagnostics: Defective
Test Steps:
1. User initiates the device scan.
2. Application performs the hardware scan.
3. Database returns the component name, impact, and diagnostics.
4. Application calculates the cost of replacement.
5. Application displays the results to the user.
Expected Result:
- User receives a report with the component name, impact, and diagnostics.
- The component is marked as defective, and the cost of replacement is calculated.

Title: Life Expectancy Calculation
Preconditions: User is logged in and has access to life expectancy calculation.
Test Data:
- Component name: ComponentC
- Health status: Good
- Speed status: Fast
Test Steps:
1. User initiates the life expectancy calculation.
2. Application performs the health and speed scan.
3. Application calculates the life expectancy.
4. Application displays the results to the user.
Expected Result:
- User receives a report with the life expectancy of the component.
- The component's life expectancy is calculated based on its health and speed status.

Title: Life Expectancy Calculation with Degraded Component
Preconditions: User is logged in and has access to life expectancy calculation.
Test Data:
- Component name: ComponentD
- Health status: Poor
- Speed status: Slow
Test Steps:
1. User initiates the life expectancy calculation.
2. Application performs the health and speed scan.
3. Application calculates the life expectancy.
4. Application displays the results to the user.
Expected Result:
- User receives a report with the life expectancy of the component.
- The component's life expectancy is calculated based on its degraded health and slow speed status.
